_comment: Adds the legacy RH-SSO 7.6.5 cluster as a trusted OIDC Identity Provider
and creates a dedicated client for the JWT Authorization Grant (RFC 7523).
NOTE the URL difference: RH-SSO 7.6 uses /auth/realms/..., RHBK 26+ uses /realms/...
Replace ALL placeholders marked with < > before importing.

clients._comment: Dedicated client for the Token Exchange Proxy to perform JWT Authorization Grants
clients.name: Token Exchange Service Client
clients.description: Used by the Token Exchange Proxy to swap RH-SSO tokens for native RHBK tokens via JWT Authorization Grant

_setup_notes.step_1: Import via Admin Console -> Realm Settings -> Action -> Partial Import, or use kcadm.sh
_setup_notes.step_2: After import, navigate to: Clients -> token-exchange-client -> Settings
_setup_notes.step_3: Scroll to 'Capability config' and enable 'JWT Authorization Grant'
_setup_notes.step_4: In 'Allowed Identity Providers for JWT Authorization Grant', select 'rhsso'
_setup_notes.step_5: Save. Verify: POST to token endpoint with grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer and an RH-SSO assertion should return an RHBK token
_setup_notes.CVE_WARNING: CVE-2026-1486: RHBK 26.4 does not verify if the IdP is enabled before issuing tokens in the jwt-authorization-grant flow. Implement strict key rotation policies until patched.
